summary: High‑energy set with explosive Eyes → Estimated and soaring Comes A Time
blurb: The show delivers a tight first set featuring standout Casey Jones, Loose Lucy and a fiery Bertha solo. The second set builds momentum with an explosive Eyes → Estimated jam that segues into a soaring Comes A Time, followed by a powerful All‑Along‑The‑Watchtower and a heartfelt Days Between. Overall the performance is energetic and tight, making it a solid entry in the 1993 run.
review: On March 27, 1993 at Albany’s Knickerbocker Arena the Dead were firing on all cylinders. The first set opened with a punchy Hell In A Bucket before moving into a blistering Bertha that showcased Jerry’s “sick” licks and a memorable solo, then rolled through tight renditions of Peggy‑O, Loose Lucy and a crowd‑pleasing Casey Jones. The second set was the show’s centerpiece: an expansive Eyes Of The World → Estimated Prophet sequence that “bounced in the back” with Phil’s bass and built into a muscular jam, seamlessly feeding into a soaring Comes A Time where Jerry’s tone sounded especially confident after his recent health scares. Highlights continued with All Along The Watchtower, a soaring Days Between that kept the audience on its feet, and a rousing One More Saturday Night before an encore of I Fought The Law that let Bill’s drums shine. Across the night the band was tight, energetic, and emotionally resonant – a standout performance from the early‑’90s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of the six recordings are strongly positive, praising the setlist flow, solos and overall energy; one negative review provides no performance details, and a mixed review still highlights strong moments. The preponderance of praise yields a solid +1.5 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, hiss‑free soundboard capture praised as “digitally flawless” with excellent mix and full coverage of the night’s standout moments.

key_highlights: Explosive Eyes → Estimated jam that fuels a seamless transition into Comes A Time.
High‑energy first set with tight playing, highlighted by Casey Jones and Loose Lucy.
Powerful Bertha solo and soaring vocal delivery on Comes A Time.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers powerful, hot solos—especially on Bertha and Comes A Time—and his vocals are emotionally resonant on the slower numbers.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, bouncing bass foundation that shines during the Estimated Prophet section and drives the band throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Adds strong rhythm guitar work and pushes forward with vocal lines despite occasional scratchiness; uses tasteful digital delay effects.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard groove is praised for its swing, though some mixes bury it; overall supports the jam sections effectively.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum mix occasionally highlights high‑hat clipping on Days Between, but his playing remains solid and contributes to the show’s drive.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is described as “quite good,” laying down a tight backbeat that fuels the high‑energy moments.
